title: cystopteris fragilis subsp. canariensis
intro: Cystopteris fragilis subsp. canariensis, also known as the Canary Island fragile fern, is a subspecies of fern native to the Canary Islands, characterized by its delicate fronds and slender stems. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Cystopteris fragilis subsp. canariensis include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a sought-after herbal remedy in the region. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the dried fronds in hot water to create a tea, or by combining them with other medicinal plants to create a poultice or tincture.
